pr.title: Print bytes to the next trophy.
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: Updated the progress calculation tool to print how many additional decompiled code bytes are needed to reach the next trophy milestone. The change was a small tooling improvement in tools/calcprogress.py, adding a bytes_to_go_next_trophy calculation based on the current trophy count and bytesPerTrophy, then reporting the value in the progress output.
what_changed: Added a bytes_to_go_next_trophy variable in tools/calcprogress.py.
Computed the next trophy threshold as ((trophyCount + 1) * bytesPerTrophy) - decomp_code_size.
Printed a new progress line: "Code bytes to go for next trophy:" followed by math.floor(bytes_to_go_next_trophy) + 1.
No source decompilation files, headers, or matching code were changed.
smart_moves: Reused the existing trophyCount, bytesPerTrophy, and decomp_code_size values instead of introducing a separate progress model.
Kept the change localized to the progress-reporting script, avoiding impact on build or matching behavior.
Reported a concrete byte target, which makes decomp progress milestones more actionable than only showing percentage and trophy count.
decomp_lessons: Small tooling improvements can improve contributor motivation by translating abstract completion percentages into the next concrete milestone.

When progress milestones are derived from floating-point ratios, converting the remaining amount to an integer display value should be done deliberately; this PR used math.floor(...)+1 for the printed byte count.
Progress scripts should reuse existing size accounting variables so displayed metrics stay consistent with the rest of the tool output.
naming_conventions: The new local variable used snake_case: bytes_to_go_next_trophy.
The surrounding script already mixed naming styles such as trophyCount, eventCount, decomp_code_size, bytesPerTrophy, and codeCompletionPcnt; this PR did not normalize those existing names.
review_feedback: No review comments, issue comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.
searchable_terms: Print bytes to the next trophy
Code bytes to go for next trophy
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to tools/calcprogress.py that adjust trophy or event milestone calculations.
Search for other PRs that improve progress reporting or contributor-facing decomp metrics.
Check whether math.floor(bytes_to_go_next_trophy)+1 was later changed or validated for edge cases at exact trophy thresholds.
